title: Hebecocca panamensis
intro: Hebecocca panamensis, also known as Panama plant, is a plant species that belongs to the family Rubiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, offering various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, wounds, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Hebecocca panamensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Hebecocca panamensis is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional remedies to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Hebecocca panamensis is a species of plant in the family Rubiaceae. This plant is commonly known as a member of the flowering plant clade, angiosperms. As a member of the clade Magnoliophyta, it belongs to the larger group of vascular plants. Hebecocca panamensis is further classified within the clade Tracheophyta and the division Magnoliophyta. Its classification also includes the kingdom Plantae.
